summary: httpOnly cookie auth migration verified end-to-end. Backend: 13/13 pytest cases green (login/me/cookie+bearer dual-read/signup/logout/session-exchange/protected endpoints/notarization gate/WebSocket). Frontend: demo + admin cookie login, session-persist-on-reload, httpOnly cookie (not JS-readable), localStorage clean, and WebSocket /api/ws/global cookie auth all verified. Only minor UI issue: cannot trigger the logout dropdown via testid in automated run (button visible but menu doesn't open on click) — backend logout endpoint itself works perfectly via API.

ui_bugs.issue: Clicking [data-testid="user-menu-trigger"] did not open the dropdown in Playwright, so logout option not reachable. May be a Radix/Headless UI dropdown that needs different interaction, or the menu is keyboard-only. Adds friction to e2e logout testing.

action_items: Optional: add a data-testid="logout-button" directly on the visible logout control (or open the user menu via a stable trigger) so automated e2e logout tests can click it. Backend logout is already verified.

Optional: investigate the four pre-login /api/auth/me 401s in console — they happen before login completes (AuthContext bootstrap) and are harmless, but could be suppressed by skipping the call when no 'cookie' sentinel exists in storage.
critical_code_review_comments: auth.py set_auth_cookie correctly sets HttpOnly + Secure + SameSite=lax + max_age=86400 + path=/. Verified via raw Set-Cookie header inspection.
get_current_user reads cookie FIRST then Authorization header — dual-read fallback works (header-only request returned 200 for /me).
POST /api/auth/logout returns 200 and emits a clearing Set-Cookie (Max-Age=0). Confirmed cookie jar invalidation by re-calling /me -> 401.
POST /api/auth/session correctly exchanges a Bearer header into the httpOnly cookie (used by SSO redirect bridge).
WS /api/ws/global authenticates via websocket.cookies['access_token'] handshake — confirmed both via pytest websockets client and via the live frontend (received {"type":"connected"} frame).
Notarization gate intact: demo@test.com (unverified) -> 403 identity_verification_required on POST /api/notary/requests.
Frontend: window.localStorage.getItem('token') is null after login; document.cookie does NOT expose 'access_token' (HttpOnly proven). Reload restores session via cookie.
Admin cookie auth works (admin login -> /admin -> admin-stats-grid visible).

success_rate.frontend: ~90% (logout UI control not click-reachable via testid; everything else green)

seed_data_creation: Created one signup user with TEST_cookie_<uuid>@example.com to verify signup cookie path; safe to ignore.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Important quirk: this preview ingress (Cloudflare) bot-challenges the default python-requests UA, producing 30s read timeouts. ALL backend tests must set a browser-like User-Agent (UA_HEADERS in test file). Also pace logins (10/min limit) by sharing module-scoped session fixtures. WebSocket tests use the `websockets` library with additional_headers=[('Cookie','access_token=...')]. Frontend logout via UI dropdown is not reachable in automation — use backend POST /api/auth/logout for E2E tests instead, or add a stable data-testid directly on the visible logout control.
rca of the issue: No bug to RCA — migration is implemented correctly per the dual-accept pattern. The only finding (logout dropdown not opening via testid in headless Playwright) is a test ergonomics gap, not a security/functional bug.
